# Mathematical Models for Wardley Mapping

> A research repository formalizing [Wardley Mapping](https://medium.com/wardleymaps) as a quantitative mathematical framework. A Wardley Map is modeled as a tuple $\mathcal{M} = (V, E, U, \nu, \varepsilon, t)$ — components $V$, dependency edges $E$, anchor set $U \subseteq V$ (one or more user-need nodes), visibility function $\nu$ (Y-axis), evolution function $\varepsilon$ (X-axis), and time $t$ — enabling computation, simulation, and data-driven strategic decision-making.

This repository contains two layers: markdown documents developing the mathematical theory (`docs/`), and a portable Claude Code skill that applies the theory to generate Wardley Maps from scenarios (`skills/wardley-map/`) with a benchmark workspace (`skills/wardley-map-workspace/`). Mathematical notation uses GitHub-native LaTeX (`$...$` inline, `$$...$$` display).

Key conventions:
- **Visibility (Y-axis)**: $\nu(v) = 1/(1+d(v))$ where $d(v)$ is graph distance from the user node. The skill uses an exponential variant $\nu(v) = e^{-0.6 \cdot d(v)}$ by default.
- **Evolution (X-axis)**: $\varepsilon(v) \in [0,1]$ mapped to stages — Genesis $[0, 0.25)$, Custom Built $[0.25, 0.5)$, Product (+rental) $[0.5, 0.75)$, Commodity (+utility) $[0.75, 1]$.
- **Evolution dynamics**: logistic S-curve $d\varepsilon/dt = r\varepsilon(1-\varepsilon)$ (labeled as a stylized extension; Wardley's climatic patterns state *"you cannot measure evolution over time or adoption"*).
- **Dependencies**: directed graph where $(a,b) \in E$ means "a depends on b", with constraint $\nu(a) \ge \nu(b)$.

## Overview

- [README](README.md): Repository overview, key formulas, and an index of all documents.
- [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md): Guidance for working with the repository, including content structure and notation conventions.

## Core mathematical model (`docs/core/`)

- [Part 1 — Core Mathematical Model](docs/core/part-1-core-model.md): The formal tuple model $\mathcal{M} = (V, E, U, \nu, \varepsilon, t)$ with computed visibility, evolution scoring, and derived decision metrics (flagged as repo-proposed heuristics, not canonical Wardley concepts).
- [Part 2 — Evolution, Not Maturity](docs/core/part-2-evolution-not-maturity.md): Refines the evolution axis interpretation, distinguishing evolution from maturity.
- [Part 3 — The Tea Shop Worked Example](docs/core/part-3-tea-shop-example.md): Applies the model to Simon Wardley's classic Tea Shop example.
- [Part 4 — Single-Component Evolution](docs/core/part-4-single-component-evolution.md): Methods for computing evolution scores for individual components.
- [Part 5 — Layer Visibility & Sigmoid Evolution](docs/core/part-5-layer-visibility-sigmoid.md): Extended model with discrete visibility layers and sigmoid evolution.
- [Part 6 — Cheat-Sheet Evolution Scoring](docs/core/part-6-cheat-sheet-scoring.md): Wardley's canonical 19-row evolution cheat sheet reproduced in full, with a formal scoring procedure that produces $\varepsilon(v)$ with an uncertainty estimate from per-row disagreement.
- [Mathematical Framework](docs/core/mathematical-framework.md): Long encyclopedic reference (1200+ lines) covering graph theory, game theory, probability, and machine learning applications. Browse for specific techniques, don't read front-to-back.

## Extensions (`docs/extensions/`)

- [Inertia](docs/extensions/inertia.md): Wardley's 17 forms of inertia (14 consumer + 3 supplier) replacing the single $c_v(t)$ drag scalar with a structured sum over severities $\iota_i$ and weights $\lambda_i$.
- [Multi-Wave Evolution](docs/extensions/multi-wave-evolution.md): Replaces Part 1's single-logistic dynamics with a multi-generation model — each component has multiple diffusion waves separated by chasms. Includes the Peace/War/Wonder cycle.
- [Component Types](docs/extensions/component-types.md): Extends the tuple with $\tau: V \to \{A, P, D, K\}$ (Activities, Practices, Data, Knowledge), giving type-dependent evolution rates ($r_A > r_P > r_D > r_K$), and formalizing the climatic pattern "practices co-evolve with activities".

## Catalogues (`docs/catalogues/`)

- [Gameplay](docs/catalogues/gameplay.md): Wardley's 61 gameplays across 12 categories (Basic Operations, User Perception, Accelerators, Deaccelerators, Dealing with Toxicity, Market, Defensive, Attacking, Ecosystem, Competitor, Positional, Poison), each with a structured mechanism mapping to $r_v, c_v, \nu, \varepsilon$ or $E$ transformations.
- [Doctrine](docs/catalogues/doctrine.md): Wardley's 40 doctrine principles organized into 4 phases (Stop Self-Destructive Behaviour, Becoming More Context Aware, Better for Less, Continuously Evolving) and 6 categories, with a math-model reading of each as a constraint on the mapping process.

## Strategy (`docs/strategy/`)

- [Strategy Cycle — Core](docs/strategy/strategy-cycle-core.md): Mathematical model of the Wardley Strategy Cycle (purpose, landscape, climate, doctrine, leadership).
- [Strategy Cycle — Example](docs/strategy/strategy-cycle-example.md): Applied worked example of the strategy cycle model.
- [Weak Signals — Core](docs/strategy/weak-signals-core.md): Mathematical framework for detecting and modeling weak signals in component evolution.
- [Weak Signals — Example](docs/strategy/weak-signals-example.md): Applied worked example of weak signal detection.
- [Strategic Mastery](docs/strategy/strategic-mastery.md): Older companion treatment of gameplay selection (predates `catalogues/gameplay.md`).
- [Gameplay Math Models](docs/strategy/gameplay-math-models.md): Older quantitative treatment of plays (predates `catalogues/gameplay.md`).

## Tools, prompts, skill

- [Wardley Map Generator Prompt](prompts/wardley_map_generator.md): AI prompt for generating Wardley Maps in OWM (Online Wardley Maps) format compatible with [create.wardleymaps.ai](https://create.wardleymaps.ai).
- [`wardley-map` Claude Code skill](skills/wardley-map/): Self-contained portable skill package. `SKILL.md` + `references/` (climatic-patterns, doctrine, evolution-stages, gameplay-patterns, inertia, mapping-examples, mathematical-models). Install to `~/.claude/skills/wardley-map/` and invoke `/wardley-map <scenario>`.
- [Benchmark workspace](skills/wardley-map-workspace/): Iteration history and a 25-map blind benchmark of the skill against Wardley's own published maps. See `BENCHMARK-REPORT.md` and `BENCHMARK-METHODOLOGY.md`.

## Articles

- [The Wardley Map Skill: What It Is, How We Tested It, What the Benchmark Found](docs/articles/wardley-map-skill-benchmark.md): Narrative write-up of the skill's five-part architecture, the blind test methodology against 25 of Wardley's own maps, the aggregate benchmark numbers (61% strategic-tolerance agreement, 92% within one band, 37% strict same-band), the coverage and time-drift caveats, and the post-benchmark v2/v3 iterations. Hero image: `docs/articles/hero.png` (generated by `hero.py`; uses the Claude palette).

## Optional

- [Wardley Maps (external, Simon Wardley's Medium book)](https://medium.com/wardleymaps): Simon Wardley's original qualitative framework, the conceptual foundation for this work.
- [Bits or pieces? (external, Simon Wardley's blog)](https://blog.gardeviance.org/): Wardley's blog with primary-source posts on climatic patterns, doctrine, inertia, and gameplay.
- [Online Wardley Maps (external)](https://onlinewardleymaps.com/): Free tool for creating Wardley Maps in OWM text format.
